channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: WHO raises Ebola public health risk to ‘very high’ in DR Congo
item.description: Provincial gov't in Ituri province, the epicentre of the outbreak, has banned public gatherings to stop spread of virus.

item.title: Ukrainian strike on dormitory in Russian-occupied Starobilsk kills four
item.description: A Ukrainian strike collapsed a five-storey college dormitory in the Russian-occupied city of Starobilsk, killing four.

item.title: Why is Trump deploying 5,000 troops to Poland?
item.description: Poland's right-wing president has welcomed Trump's announcement.

item.title: Marco Rubio visits India: What’s at stake with Trump-Modi ties strained?
item.description: Rubio is taking an extended trip ahead of next week's Quad meeting of foreign ministers.

item.title: Turkiye shutters liberal Istanbul university seized in criminal probe
item.description: Council of Higher Education promised to take measures to ensure Bilgi University students 'suffer no harm'.

item.title: Russia labels Ukraine attack in occupied Luhansk ‘monstrous crime’
item.description: Moscow says student dormitory hit, killing at least four people.

item.title: Senegal PM slams West’s ‘homosexual tyranny’, defends LGBTQ crackdown
item.description: Human rights groups have criticised the government's legislation targeting same-sex relationships.

item.title: South Korean activists smash Starbucks cups to protest ‘Tank Day’ campaign
item.description: South Korean activists smash Starbucks cups to protest ‘Tank Day’ campaign

item.title: Israeli attacks kill several in Lebanon, with health workers targeted
item.description: More than 400 people have been killed since the ceasefire came into effect in mid-April.

item.title: Russia ‘looking for new soldiers’; Kyiv claims 83,000 dead so far in 2026
item.description: Top Ukrainian officials claim Moscow's woes are growing as independent estimates suggest Russia's economy is faltering.

item.title: How Guardiola compares with Ferguson and other English managerial greats
item.description: Pep Guardiola won 17 trophies in his 10-year stay at Man City, but how does his record stand up in English football?

item.title: What is Hindutva, and what are the roots of this political movement?
item.description: Hindutva is very different from Hinduism. One is a 20th century majoritarian movement, the other an ancient faith.

item.title: India is being left to die in the heat
item.description: Modi denied climate change for years. Now, as heat deaths mount, his government offers branding instead of protection.

item.title: Everest record holder warns of Nepal danger as two Indian mountaineers die
item.description: At least 5 climbers have died during this Everest season, including the 2 Indians and 3 Nepalis.

item.title: Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile: Can it be safely transferred?
item.description: Reports say Khamenei issued a directive that Iran's enriched uranium should not be sent abroad.

item.title: Man City confirm Guardiola exit to end decade-long, trophy-laden spell
item.description: Pep Guardiola is standing down as Manchester City manager after winning 20 trophies with the Premier League club.

item.title: British climber sets record with 20th Everest summit
item.description: Kenton Cool is the first non-Nepali to have 20 Everest summits under his belt.

item.title: Gang violence kills at least 25 in Honduras
item.description: The attacks by gunmen come as the government continues a drive to crack down on organised crime.

item.title: Man Utd appoint Michael Carrick as permanent manager
item.description: Carrick steps up from role as interim coach to replace Ruben Amorim on a permanent basis as Manchester United manager.

item.title: Hundreds protest Ireland’s ‘George Floyd moment’
item.description: ndreds protest Ireland’s ‘George Floyd moment’

item.title: Can Venezuelan oil save India amid the Hormuz energy crisis?
item.description: Venezuelan Acting President Delcy Rodriguez is expected to travel to India next week to discuss oil sales.

item.title: ‘Something big taking place’: Why has Russia sent more nukes to Belarus?
item.description: Russia's ally participated in joint drills for the first time. Analysts say it was a risky move for Minsk.

item.title: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa says he will leave role after poor season
item.description: Arbeloa says he will not be coaching the team next season, amid reports that Jose Mourinho is returning to the club.

item.title: Tuchel leaves Palmer and Foden out of England’s 2026 World Cup squad
item.description: Real Madrid's Trent Alexander-Arnold also left out, while Saudi-based Al-Ahli striker Ivan Toney the surprise inclusion.

item.title: US deepens European uncertainty with deployment of 5,000 troops to Poland
item.description: Erratic US policy moves leave European NATO partners increasingly unclear about which areas they should prioritise.
